rxn_class_summary: NaBr-mediated electrochemical anti-Markovnikov hydration of aryl alkenes to β-aryl ethanols via an aryl epoxide intermediate
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Br- to Br2/[Br]+ which acts as mediator to brominate the alkene; cathodic reduction of H2O generates OH-/H2 and later reduces the aryl epoxide to the anti-Markovnikov alcohol

key_intermediates.name: benzylic ketyl/α-oxy radical (after cathodic 1e- reduction of epoxide)

electron_flow: During constant-current electrolysis, anode oxidizes 2 Br- to Br2 which disproportionates with H2O to [Br]+/HBr; [Br]+ adds to the aryl alkene to form a cyclic bromonium that is opened by water to give a β-bromoalcohol, which is converted to an aryl epoxide by OH- (generated at the cathode from water reduction). Under constant-voltage electrolysis, the aryl epoxide is cathodically reduced (1e-, then protonation, then 1e-) to deliver the anti-Markovnikov β-aryl ethanol.

probe_reagents.name: aliphatic alkene (1al) control
probe_reagents.observation: Yielded only the epoxide 4al (60%) with no anti-Markovnikov alcohol, indicating aryl group is required for cathodic epoxide reduction

probe_reagents.name: isolated aryl epoxide intermediate
probe_reagents.observation: Epoxide isolated and shown to be reduced to the β-aryl ethanol product, supporting it as an on-cycle intermediate

notes: Mechanism is sequential paired electrolysis carried out in two stages (constant-current then constant-voltage). Alternative direct hydration pathways via benzylic carbocation or radical-polar crossover are ruled out implicitly by the requirement of the aryl epoxide intermediate; aliphatic substrates stop at the epoxide stage because their epoxides are not reducible under the cathodic conditions.
